does: This paper surveys 194 papers on ChatGPT and related GPT models, conducting trend analysis, word cloud representation, and distribution analysis across application domains. It documents the landscape of ChatGPT-related research following the release of GPT-3.5 and GPT-4, identifying key innovations including large-scale pre-training on web-scale data, instruction fine-tuning, and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) as drivers of improved adaptability and performance. The survey reveals that research interest concentrates primarily on direct natural language processing applications while spanning diverse domains including education, mathematics, medicine, and physics, providing a comprehensive overview of ChatGPT's demonstrated capabilities and implications across multiple fields.
connects: This work builds on and synthesizes the emerging research wave following OpenAI's release of ChatGPT in late 2022, which itself represents a culmination of prior advances in large-scale language modeling, transformer architectures, and instruction-following techniques. It connects to established lines of work on pre-training methodology, transfer learning in NLP, and human-in-the-loop model improvement through RLHF—techniques developed through GPT-2, GPT-3, and subsequent refinements. The survey responds to and organizes the explosion of papers investigating how these state-of-the-art models could be applied, fine-tuned, evaluated, and integrated across domains, thereby tracing how foundational advances in language model scaling and alignment translate into practical downstream applications.
enables: This comprehensive survey establishes a structured understanding of the ChatGPT research landscape that can guide researchers in identifying gaps, overlaps, and promising directions for future work. By mapping application domains and detecting emerging areas with less coverage, it enables more targeted investigation of LLM potential in underexplored fields such as specialized scientific domains. The synthesis of ethical concerns and implications supports the development of responsible deployment frameworks and governance approaches. This foundational mapping enables researchers to position novel contributions within the broader ecosystem, informs decisions about which application areas merit deeper investigation, and provides evidence for understanding how large-scale, instruction-tuned language models reshape research practices across disciplines.
